destinations.evidence: LLM has_error responses are turned into a prefixed decode error that compute_llm_executor_fee recognizes as a settlement-preserving constant-fee path.
The constant executor fee is exactly 500,000,000,000 wei and is bounded at compile time against the HTTP escrow floor.
destinations.snippet:
pub const LLM_ERROR_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI: u128 = 500_000_000_000;
const _: () = assert!(LLM_ERROR_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI <= HTTP_EXECUTOR_BASE_FEE_WEI, ...);

equivalence_argument: The port preserves the has_error settlement path: decode emits the dedicated prefix and fee computation returns the fixed executor fee instead of failing settlement.
notes: This maps the executor-error fee path; zero token metrics are implicit because fee computation returns before normal metric pricing.
rationale: The diff adds the constant error fee and a dedicated decode-error branch for `LLM response flagged error`, returning the fee and empty pricing metrics so the on-chain settlement can expose the error response. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When LLM response decoding detects an executor response with `has_error=true`, fee calculation should return `LLM_ERROR_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI` of 500,000,000,000 wei with zero token metrics instead of treating the response as an undecodable pricing failure that drops settlement.
suggested_falsification_tests: Construct an LLM response ABI with `has_error=true` and an error message, run LLM fee calculation, and assert it returns `500_000_000_000` wei, zero prompt/completion metrics, and no hard decode failure.
summary: LLM has_error responses settle with the constant executor fee.
test_or_falsification_candidate: ABI-encode an LLM response with has_error=true and assert compute_llm_executor_fee returns 500_000_000_000 wei.
title: LLM error responses settle with constant executor fee
